name: Moong Dal Khichdi
tagline: One-pot rice and split-yellow lentil — gentle, low-purine, kidney-kind
shortTagline: rice + dal · easy on stomach · 35 min
highlight: Single-pot, low-purine — a clinical favourite for high uric acid and kidney stress

hero.alt: Soft yellow rice-and-lentil khichdi in a steel bowl with ghee and cumin tadka
hero.caption: moong dal khichdi — soft, soothing, ghee-finished

healthFacts: Split moong is the lowest-purine pulse — gentle on uric acid and kidney filtration.
Rice + dal pairing forms a complete protein without straining BUN/creatinine.
Soft, cooked-down texture is easier to digest than whole grains during recovery.
Cumin and ghee tadka adds satiety without heavy salt or saturated fat.

steps.title: Wash and pressure-cook
steps.detail: Rinse rice and dal together until water runs clear. Add to a pressure cooker with 3.5 cups water, turmeric, and salt. Pressure cook for 4 whistles on medium and let pressure release naturally.
steps.tip: Soaking 15 min before cooking gives a creamier finish.

steps.title: Make the cumin tadka
steps.detail: Heat ghee in a small pan. Add cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 20 seconds. Drop in grated ginger and turn off the heat the moment it turns fragrant.
steps.tip: No garlic / hing keeps the dish strictly low-purine and stomach-friendly.

steps.title: Combine and serve
steps.detail: Open the cooker, mash lightly to a porridge-like consistency, pour the tadka over the top. Adjust salt. Serve hot with a spoon of dahi and lime wedge.
steps.tip: If too thick, stir in a splash of hot water — it should be soft, not stiff.
